design_philosophy.core_loop: Command a four-tank platoon through real-time tactical battles; switch between tactical map oversight and first-person control of individual tank roles (commander, driver, gunner) to manage interdependent combat arms
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Asymmetric warfare—technologically superior NATO forces (M1 Abrams tanks) versus numerically superior Soviet/Warsaw Pact forces across a fictional Central European campaign starting defensively then shifting offensive
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Armored platoon commander experiencing authentic tank warfare with crew progression and strategic depth; ability to toggle between commanding overview and visceral first-person gunner/driver perspectives
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game demands terrain mastery (hull-down positioning as critical survival strategy) and combined arms coordination—supporting units (Bradleys, helicopters, MLRS, dragons) must be carefully positioned and rotated to avoid artillery punishment. Rejects arcade tank action in favor of deliberate, strategic gameplay where crew survival directly impacts unit capability through rank/skill advancement

difficulty.lives_system: Crew members can die permanently; surviving crew accumulate rank and skill between missions, incentivizing player to keep team alive
difficulty.checkpointing: Campaign-based mission structure; progress through scenarios contingent on successful objectives
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can adjust engagement style from action-oriented (direct tank control focus) to strategy-oriented (tactical map command focus); mission support units vary by scenario, providing flexible resource management options

progression.upgrade_system: Crew ranking and skill advancement—surviving crew members increase in military rank and skill between battles, providing persistent stat progression and incentive for crew preservation
progression.unlock_mechanism: Campaign progression follows fictional Rhine crossing scenario; early missions are defensive (stemming Soviet advances), success enables later offensive missions with waypoint objectives
replayability_hooks: Crew permadeath and skill advancement creates unique playthroughs based on crew survival
Multiple difficulty approaches (action vs. strategy focus) support different playstyles
Tactical flexibility with varying support units per mission allows different combined arms solutions

world_building_techniques: Fictional Cold War scenario set in Central Europe with Warsaw Pact vs. NATO framing
Campaign structure (Rhine crossing, defensive-to-offensive arc) provides strategic context
Realistic military vehicles and doctrine (M1 Abrams, M2 Bradley, MLRS, M47 Dragon) ground narrative in plausible conflict
Crew progression narrative: rank advancement ties gameplay success to character development

faction_indicators: NATO vs. Warsaw Pact ideological divide
U.S. M1 Abrams as symbol of technological superiority
Soviet numerical advantage as strategic pressure
Central European setting as Cold War flashpoint
danger_signals: Artillery barrages punish static unit positioning
Numerically superior enemy forces create existential pressure on NATO forces
Crew permadeath raises stakes of tactical decisions
environmental_storytelling: Terrain importance (hull-down positions, cover utilization) reflects authentic armor doctrine
Multi-unit support coordination (infantry disembarking to use anti-tank launchers) demonstrates interdependent combat operations
Mission progression from defensive Rhine crossing to offensive operations tells strategic narrative arc
